questions.question: The main function of nitrogen in plants is
questions.explanation: Nitrogen promotes vegetative growth and is essential for leaf and stem development.

questions.question: Crop rotation is important because it
questions.explanation: Crop rotation helps maintain soil fertility by preventing nutrient depletion.

questions.question: The ideal soil pH for most crops is
questions.explanation: Most crops prefer slightly acidic to neutral soil with pH 6.0-7.0.

questions.question: Which of these is a legume crop?
questions.explanation: Beans are legumes that fix nitrogen in the soil through root nodules.

questions.question: Organic matter in soil improves
questions.explanation: Organic matter improves both water retention and soil drainage properties.

questions.question: Mulching in agriculture serves to
questions.explanation: Mulching conserves soil moisture and suppresses weed growth.

questions.question: The process by which plants manufacture food is
questions.explanation: Photosynthesis is the process where plants convert sunlight into food energy.

questions.question: Which nutrient deficiency causes chlorosis?
questions.explanation: Iron deficiency causes chlorosis (yellowing of leaves).

questions.question: Sustainable agriculture aims to
options: maintain productivity without degrading the environment
questions.explanation: Sustainable agriculture balances productivity with environmental protection.

questions.question: The practice of growing two crops on the same field in one year is called
questions.explanation: Double cropping involves growing two crops sequentially on the same field in one year.
